name: Savvy Ladies
description_short: Savvy Ladies is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing free financial guidance, education, and helpline support exclusively to women nationwide.
description_long:
Savvy Ladies was founded in 2003 as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering women through financial education and guidance. The organization has grown to serve over 35,000 women across all 50 states and DC, operating a volunteer-powered model that connects women with financial professionals offering free advice. Founded by Stacy Francis, Savvy Ladies explicitly targets reducing economic and racial disparities in financial education access for women. The organization's mission centers on helping women take control of their finances and make informed, confident financial decisions.

Savvy Ladies offers a comprehensive suite of free financial services including a helpline (which has exceeded 7,000 women submitting questions), free financial courses, webinar sessions, a financial well-being workbook, and a podcast called "Power of the Ask." They provide topic-specific guidance through a Financial Roadmap covering budgeting, career and income, caregiving, debt management, divorce and money, family finances, financial abuse recovery, investing, marriage, midlife finances, retirement, and widowhood. The helpline is staffed by 200+ volunteer financial professionals who provide monthly guidance to over 200 women. All services are completely free with no fees, products to purchase, or credit-based eligibility requirements.

What distinguishes Savvy Ladies is its exclusive focus on women's financial empowerment and its reliance on a diverse volunteer network of financial professionals rather than paid staff advisors. The organization provides specialized guidance on women-specific financial situations like divorce and money, widowhood, financial abuse recovery, and caregiving responsibilities. Their volunteer model creates a peer-professional dynamic while maintaining non-profit integrity. The organization operates nationally across 50 states plus DC, making services accessible regardless of geographic location.

Savvy Ladies is legitimately valuable for women seeking free financial guidance with no strings attached. However, the volunteer-based model means response times may vary, and guidance quality could fluctuate depending on volunteer expertise. As a nonprofit helpline rather than professional financial planning, they may not provide the depth of personalized planning available through fee-based advisors, though this is inherent to their free model. The organization is well-established and transparent about its nonprofit status and mission.
pros: Completely free financial helpline and courses with no fees or hidden costs
200+ volunteer financial professionals available monthly across all 50 states plus DC
Specialized guidance for women-specific situations: divorce, widowhood, financial abuse recovery, caregiving
Established 501(c)(3) nonprofit since 2003 with track record serving 35,000+ women
Multiple free formats: helpline, courses, webinars, podcast, workbook, and blog
No credit requirements or financial eligibility screening to access services
Mission explicitly addresses economic and racial disparities in financial education
cons: Volunteer-based model means response times and expertise quality may vary by volunteer
Free guidance may lack the depth and personalized planning of paid financial advisors
Helpline format limits ability to handle complex, ongoing portfolio management or tax planning
Geographically distributed volunteers may have limited knowledge of state-specific financial laws
As a helpline model, may not provide hands-on implementation support or accountability tracking
best_for: Women seeking free financial guidance with no debt or income restrictions
Women navigating life transitions: divorce, widowhood, career changes, retirement
Women affected by financial abuse recovering and rebuilding financial independence
Women in caregiving roles managing family finances alongside care responsibilities
diagnosis: Savvy Ladies is ideal for women seeking free, non-judgmental financial guidance from vetted professionals without sales pressure or debt-based qualification requirements. The main caveat is that volunteer-based helpline services cannot replace comprehensive financial planning from credentialed advisors, and response quality depends on individual volunteer expertise—but this is a reasonable trade-off for a completely free, woman-focused nonprofit service.
services: Free Financial Helpline for women with 200+ volunteer advisors
Free Financial Courses on various personal finance topics
Financial Roadmap with topic guidance (debt, divorce, investing, retirement, etc.)
Blog with Stacy's Savvy Financial Advice
Free Panel Series on financial topics
